findings.note: The Claude-path guard is driven with the payload in a PAYLOAD environment variable (PAYLOAD=... node sdd-hook-guard.js --emit exit) rather than on stdin, while the staged claude-hooks.json implies stdin delivery under real Claude Code hook dispatch. The .js twin's stdin parsing of Bash payloads is only exercised indirectly (the sh dispatcher path routes stdin to the python3 twin). Non-blocking because the identical stdin invocation form is already load-bearing for the existing Edit/Write/MultiEdit routing and exit-code-2 deny semantics match Claude hook semantics, but a stdin-delivered .js case would close the fidelity gap.

Requirements edge cases enumerate three malformed shapes (missing tool_name, missing tool_input, non-JSON stdin); the corpus covers missing tool_input and non-JSON but omits the missing-tool_name shape. Fail-closed behavior is still demonstrated for two shapes across all three hook paths.

HOOKS_JSON path is redacted in the green and gate-time rerun logs, so the bundle alone cannot distinguish whether the passing rerun exercised the live plugins/sdd-quality-loop/hooks/claude-hooks.json (post human-copy) or the staged human-copy file. Acceptable given the pre-gate workflow state and the SHA-256 manifest (manifest hash 09c76c5c... matches the staged artifact), but the quality gate should confirm the live file hash after the human copy.
